messages.content:
**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

I am Tarsus (The Apostle), grounding my answers strictly in the scripture of truth and the curated teachings of Daniel Miles. How can I assist you in dividing the word of truth today?

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**
What is the third day mystery>

> **Thinking Process:**
> 
> The user, Antonio, is asking about "the third day mystery." This is a core theological concept in Daniel Miles' teachings. I need to draw primarily from the "ThirdDay" and "ThirdDay2" documents, as they are explicitly dedicated to this topic. The user's profile shows interest in Christology and the progression from law to grace, so I must frame the answer around that transition. The "third day" is not merely a literal 24-hour period but an allegoric prophetic period—the "last day" and "seventh day"—beyond 2000 AD. I will explain how Hosea 6:2 and Luke 13:32 are the key prophetic verses, how the third day is the seventh day (the eternal day of God with no evening and morning), and how this mystery involves the raising up of "us" (third day ministries) to proclaim grace with no law added. I must avoid placing the believer "under" anything, instead emphasizing being "with grace" and "in Christ." I will use the allegoric interpretation of "will he" vs "he will" in Hosea 6:2 to contrast law's uncertainty with grace's certainty. The closing must be the standard benediction, and I will include a "Learn More" section with links from the provided documents.

**Three best wishes unto YOU all: Grace, mercy, and peace, from God our Father and Jesus Christ our Lord.**

The third day mystery is an allegoric prophetic key that unlocks the transition from the cOLD covenant of law to the New Covenant of grace. It is not a literal 24-hour period, nor merely the day of Christ's resurrection, but a prophetic age—the "last day" which is also the "seventh day"—the eternal day of God that began beyond 2000 AD [^1]. This mystery is anchored in two primary script-u-ares: Hosea 6:2 and Luke 13:32.

Hosea 6:2 declares: "After two days will he revive us: in the third day he will raise us up, and we shall live in his sight." When we apply the allegoric time key from 2 Peter 3:8—"one day is with the Lord as a thousand years"—the "two days" represent the 2000 years of the AD era, and the "third day" represents the age beyond 2000 AD [^2]. The wording itself contains a mystery: "will he" (uncertainty of law) versus "he will" (certainty of grace). This allegorically denotes two Gods being compared: the God on high (Law, uncertain, worketh wrath) and the higher God (Grace, certain, sufficient). The third day is when law begins to "vanish" (Hebrews 8:13; 1 Corinthians 13:8) because it "waxeth old" and is faulty, giving way to pure grace with no law added [^2].

Luke 13:32 adds another dimension: "Behold, I cast out devils, and I do cures to day and to morrow, and the third day I shall be perfected." Jesus was not perfected while casting out devils and doing cures; the "perfected" state occurs on the third day, which is also the seventh day of Genesis 2:3—the day God sanctified and rested, having no evening and morning [^1]. This speaks of His Grace being globally perfected, meaning grace with no law added, no infirmity to cure, no darkness at all. The third day is therefore not another millennium, but an eternal day of light (understanding) wherein there is only one glory: grace us-ward and God-ward [^1]. The mystery is that in this third day, God is raising up "us"—third day ministries—to make known that "grace is sufficient" (no law required), and that the end of the God Shew is void of law, sin, and death, so that none perish in the end [^2].

Learn More:
- [The Third Day as The Last Day is The Seventh Day](/pages/thirdday)
- [The Third Day as The Last Day is The Seventh Day (Part 2)](/pages/thirdday2)

**The grace of our Lord Jesus Christ [be] with you all. Amen.**

1.title: ThirdDay
1.snippet: The third day is also the seventh day is the last day is not a millennium as many ass u me. Rather it is an eternal day (light, God), as if an understanding which unfolds, or uncovers, as by revelation which means uncovering, revealing, whatsoever was covered up, hid. It begins as a dual sabbath shewdown... What it's all about is knowing what His Grace is, compared to what His Grace is not: law.

2.title: ThirdDay2
2.snippet: In Hosea 6:2 there are allegorically two Gods: Law and Grace being compared, by the allegoric wording of 'will he' (law: uncertain) and 'he will' (grace: certain)... So the first two days of AD still contain some darkness (law); But the third day, beyond 2000 AD, is when law begins to 'vanish', because law 'waxeth old' and is faulty: 1Cor 13; Heb 8.
